TRANSCRIPT:
OK, now I want to go a little bit deeper into your avatar. For this next set of
questions, I really want you to think about your greatest case study, either now or in
the future. In other words, who is just the perfect case study for you? If they don't
exist yet, then imagine that case study. Who are they going to be?
For me, I have lots of them. I've mentioned John Gallagher already, he's a
fantastic case study. The guy was on food stamps, and now he's got this sizable
business. From food stamps to six figures, and now it's beyond that. He's got a team,
he's making a huge contribution to the world, has a great business, so he's a great
case study - but I've got a whole bunch of them.
For this I want you to think about your greatest case study, somebody you've
already worked with, gotten great results for, or going to have in the future if you're
just starting your business. Who’s that case study going to be? Make a fictional
case study. I need you to go there, to think about this person, it will really help you
in this exercise going forward.
Remember, when you’re selling something it's about the transformation in
their life, about the change, the before and after. Think about before they got your
product and after.
Before John Gallagher got my product he was on food stamps. He had 2
young kids, was working at a non-profit, going to school to become an acupuncturist,
and trying to raise his kids. He and his wife Kimberly were crazy busy and couldn't
make ends meet, but now he's got this business.
He gets to do exactly what he wants all the time: he moved to exactly where
he wanted to, his kids go to the schools he wants them to go to, and he’s in my
platinum program so he's enjoying my high-end coaching and gets to hang out with
me. I've actually gone and stayed at his house, in his guest house.
He's had this amazing transformation, I can think about that
transformation. When he bought PLF from me, back in those days it was CDs and
DVDs. When he went through PLF he didn't get a book, he got a
transformation in his life. No matter what you're selling, no matter what you’re
going to launch, you’re selling a transformation as well. You're transforming how

What is the transformation in their life because of your product? And not just

If all of a sudden you've taught them how to have a home yoga practice, their

different compared to what it is now?
You're changing the future for your people, and you really want to get focused

This is going to help you as you move forward with everything you do, so look

concrete as possible.
When I just talked about the changes in John's life, from food

kids go to the schools that he wants them to go to, he gets to enjoy my

high-end coaching program, he works with top experts in his field. All

OK next, this is for the other side. What will it cost your avatar to not
have your product? What's the extra pain and frustration in their life? How much
money have they given up, or have they lost? What will they miss out on? Again, look
into their future, a year from now, 5 years from now, 10 years from now.
Go back to John Gallagher - he had spent and borrowed $20,000 from his
dad to get those initial board games made up. He tried to launch it on his own and
only sold 12 of them. He literally had palettes and boxes of these filling up his entire
house and garage. If he never sold those, how soul crushing would that be?
If 6 or 8 years later he's still got all that inventory sitting there, thinking he
borrowed all this money, this huge albatross around his neck. He wouldn't even be
able to move forward. Think about that pain, seeing those boxes in the house every
day, year after year. He had this dream, and he borrowed all this money for this
dream. It just was an utter failure.
Instead, he's sold all those games and ordered more and has now sold 50,000
of them. But he would've missed out on all of that, instead he'd just have the pain and
frustration of this dream that didn't come to pass, that was mocking him, laughing at
him.
Think about that pain and frustration for your avatar if they don't change
their life based on what you're going to do for them. Again, look forward into their
future, a year, 5 years, 10 years, 20 years, and be as specific and concrete as possible.
Now, who will your avatar become as the result of the positive
impact of your product? How are they transformed? John has become this
amazing leader. He's become almost like a guru in his market. He's become the go-to
person for other gurus to launch their products. He's become the pivotal person.
When he goes to a convention about edible and medicinal plants and herbs, everyone
knows who he is. People are walking up to him and talking to him, telling him about
how his products have helped them.
Think about whether they become a better parent, brother or sister, friend -
do they become a pillar of their society? Look into their future, and again, be
as specific and concrete as you can. Take your time when you do this
work. I can talk about it somewhat eloquently because I've been doing
